title: Northeastern University president’s compensation exceeds $3 million, records show
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Northeastern University President Joseph Aoun's compensation exceeded $3 million in Fiscal Year 2025, making him the highest-paid college leader in Massachusetts.
Joseph Aoun's pay increased sharply from $1.27 million in FY21 to $2.7 million in FY22, driven by deferred compensation and bonuses.
Aoun's earnings surpass MIT President Sally Kornbluth's, whose compensation was $2,151,788, making them the only Massachusetts presidents above $2 million.

title: Cambridge man killed in early-morning shooting, investigators say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Xavier Bautista, 32, of Cambridge, was found dead from a gunshot wound on Saturday morning at Broadway and Norfolk Street.
Police determined Bautista was shot shortly before 4:30 a.m.; his body was discovered by a pedestrian around 5:30 a.m.
Cambridge police, State Police detectives, and the DA's office are investigating and urge anyone with information to contact them.

title: New England Aquarium trainer joins effort to save endangered penguin chicks in South Africa
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: New England Aquarium's Diana Major volunteered in Cape Town to assist with penguin chick care and egg incubation.
African penguins are critically endangered, having lost 97% of their population, possibly facing extinction by 2035.
The Aquarium participates in the AZA's SAFE program, supporting conservation and rehabilitation efforts for African penguins.

title: Red Sox’ Willson Contreras apologizes for recent on-field confrontations: ‘It’s been a really tough week’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox's Willson Contreras apologizes for on-field confrontations, citing emotional stress from recent events.
Contreras and Nationals pitcher Cade Cavalli both received seven-game suspensions after a bench-clearing brawl.
Venezuelan earthquakes heavily impacted Contreras, affecting his emotional state during recent baseball games.

title: Red Sox Aroldis Chapman and Ranger Suarez selected to MLB All-Star Game
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox pitchers Aroldis Chapman and Ranger Suarez selected for 2026 MLB All-Star Game.
Chapman has a 2.10 ERA with 33 strikeouts and 17 saves in 25.2 innings this season.
Suarez has a 2.94 ERA and 92 strikeouts in 88.2 innings, returning to Philadelphia for the All-Star Game.

title: Paul Pierce encourages Jaylen Brown after trade from Celtics: ‘This is what keeps you hungry’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Paul Pierce expressed disappointment but not surprise over Jaylen Brown's trade from the Celtics to the 76ers.
Pierce encouraged Brown to use feelings of disrespect as motivation, referencing past exclusions from Olympic and All-NBA honors.
Brown criticized Celtics' management for the trade, mentioning a perceived lack of respect, while Pierce remains hopeful for Boston's future.

title: Photos: Boston celebrates the Fourth of July
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Boston celebrated the Fourth of July with a parade and a reading of the Declaration of Independence.
Festivities included the Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular, featuring a drone show and performances from artists like Chance The Rapper.
Boston Mayor Michelle Wu participated in the event, carrying her daughter to the reading outside the Old State House.

title: Is Caleb Durbin’s turnaround enough to solidify his future with the Red Sox?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Caleb Durbin leads the Red Sox with a .977 OPS and seven home runs in the last 30 days.
Durbin's improvement follows working with a new hitting coach, reversing his early season struggles.
Red Sox have team control of Durbin until 2031, potentially easing future salary cap concerns.

title: Red Sox’ Aroldis Chapman breaks MLB relief pitcher strikeout record in save vs. Angels
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Aroldis Chapman broke the MLB relief pitcher strikeout record with his 1,364th strikeout against the Los Angeles Angels.
Chapman achieved the record in 847 innings, surpassing Hoyt Wilhelm's 1,363 strikeouts over 1,872.1 innings.
The Red Sox celebrated Chapman's achievement with a video montage and presented him with the historic strikeout ball.

title: Allstate names the most collision-prone cities in the U.S.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston ranks as the most collision-prone city in the U.S., with drivers averaging 3.7 years between crashes.
Worcester and Springfield, MA, also rank among the bottom five cities for driver collision rates in the U.S.
Brownsville, Texas, records the safest driving conditions, with nearly 15 years between car collisions for motorists.
